question: Have you ever had a security incident? Walk me through your incident response process.
title: Incident History Transparency, Response Capability, and Continuous Improvement
bullets: Disclose incident history (including minor/near-miss events) with timelines and impact
Describe incident response lifecycle (detect, triage, contain, eradicate, recover, post-incident review)
Provide metrics and SLAs (MTTD, MTTR, customer notification timelines, severity classification)
Demonstrate readiness (tabletop exercises, IR retainer, playbooks) and lessons learned with concrete improvements
suggested_inputs: Incident response (IR) playbook/runbooks with severity definitions and escalation criteria
Metrics dashboard (MTTD/MTTR by severity, last 12–24 months)
Evidence of tabletop exercises and third-party IR retainer details

good.text: We transparently disclose incident history (including minor incidents and near-misses) with timelines, scope, and customer impact, along with what was learned.
Our IR process follows a defined lifecycle (detect, triage, contain, eradicate, recover) with SLAs and metrics such as MTTD/MTTR by severity, and clear customer notification timelines.
We maintain detailed IR playbooks and run quarterly tabletop exercises. We have a 24/7 IR contact and a third-party incident response retainer for major events.
Each incident results in a root cause analysis (RCA), corrective actions, and tracked improvements. We can show how controls were strengthened based on past events and threat intelligence.

partial.text: We report no major incidents and have an IR plan, but provide limited detail on metrics, exercises, or real-world handling.
We describe the incident response steps, but do not provide clear SLAs, MTTD/MTTR metrics, or notification timelines.
We acknowledge incidents in general terms, but avoid specifics on impact, timelines, or lessons learned.
We have an IR plan but do not regularly test it or lack a third-party IR retainer.

bad.text: We have never had a security incident.
We cannot discuss incidents for legal reasons.
Responds defensively or avoids providing details about incident handling.

follow_ups: What are your MTTD and MTTR by severity over the last 12 months?
Who is the 24/7 incident response contact and escalation chain?
Provide your IR playbook and define escalation criteria for P0/P1 incidents.
What is your customer notification SLA for security incidents?
Describe a recent incident or near-miss and the improvements made afterward.
What threat intelligence-driven improvements have you implemented in the last 12 months?
